clinical_history: Elderly woman with lightheadedness and slow pulse. What is the rhythm? (Note the baseline artifact.)
diagnosis: Sinus rhythm (with artifact) is present at an atrial rate of about 75/min (with possible biatrial abnormality), well seen in lead II and V1 rhythm strips. Complete AV block is present with an escape pacemaker at a regular rate of about 33 beats/min. No relationship exists between the P waves and QRS complexes. The wide QRS complexes have a right bundle branch block/left anterior fascicular block morphology consistent with severe infra-nodal disease. Prior anterior MI cannot be excluded. There is probable left ventricular hypertrophy (history of hypertension; not aortic stenosis). The prominent T wave inversions may associated with high-degree AV block, and/or due to ischemia/MI, etc. Cardiac enzyme biomarkers for acute MI were negative. No reversible causes of AV block were found. The patient underwent dual chamber pacemaker implantation.
